lesson.title: My first learning turn
lesson.subject: Lumo Fundamentals
lesson.readinessFocus: Learner-name confidence, greeting-to-answer continuity, and a complete offline startup ritual.
lesson.scenario: The learner finishes the bundled onboarding sequence by greeting Mallam, choosing the right starter card, saying their own name, and closing with a full first-turn phrase that the facilitator can reuse offline.

activitySteps.title: Open your first turn
activitySteps.prompt: Mallam says, Hello, [learner first name]. Say hello back.
activitySteps.hint: Use the learner's first name warmly, then pause before repeating.
activitySteps.detail: Makes the greeting personal before the learner speaks.
activitySteps.evidence: Learner returns the greeting in any clear form.
activitySteps.successFeedback: Good. Mallam and the learner have opened the first full turn.
activitySteps.retryFeedback: Wave once and say hello together again.
facilitatorNotes: Use the learner's first name naturally.

activitySteps.title: Pick who will speak
activitySteps.hint: Name each choice once: Mallam, listen, smile.
activitySteps.detail: Returns to the core character identification before the learner answers.
facilitatorNotes: Keep it quick so the learner stays in the dialogue rhythm.

activitySteps.title: Say your name clearly
activitySteps.prompt: Mallam asks, What is your name? Say: My name is [learner name].
activitySteps.hint: Touch the name card and model only the first two words if needed.
activitySteps.detail: Learner gives the personal introduction that anchors the full onboarding flow.
activitySteps.evidence: Learner attempts My name is ... with their own name.
facilitatorNotes: Let the learner own the answer.

activitySteps.title: Choose the start signal
activitySteps.prompt: Tap the card that says, I am ready to learn.
activitySteps.hint: Read both cards aloud once before the learner chooses.
activitySteps.detail: Turns the learner's spoken readiness into a visible start signal.
facilitatorNotes: The goal is startup recognition, not reading fluency.

activitySteps.title: Say the full first-turn phrase
activitySteps.prompt: Say: Hello, Mallam. My name is [learner name]. I am ready to learn.
activitySteps.hint: Point to the hello card, then the name card, then the ready card while the learner echoes the full line.
activitySteps.detail: Learner closes the bundled onboarding pack with one complete, reusable first-turn phrase.
activitySteps.evidence: Learner attempts the full phrase with support if needed.
expectedAnswers: Hello, Mallam. My name is [learner name]. I am ready to learn.
My name is [learner name]. I am ready to learn.
facilitatorNotes: This is the closing ritual for the offline onboarding pack.
Finish with praise and move directly into the next subject only if the learner still has energy.

items.text: Hello, Mallam. My name is [learner name]. I am ready to learn.
items.label: Full first turn phrase
